is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of P. falciparum parasitemia and mild malaria attack in Burkina Faso.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Study focuses on human hemoglobin genotypes (HbA, HbS, HbC) and host genetic protection; no P. falciparum drug-resistance markers (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfdhps, Pfk13, Pfmdr1, Pfcytb) are examined.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Only human hemoglobin allele frequencies reported (HbA 0.814, HbC 0.139, HbS 0.047); no parasite marker frequencies.

sub_country_location.evidence: Samples from urban district of Bobo-Dioulasso, Burkina Faso — sub-national location.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical samples from families living in Bobo-Dioulasso, Burkina Faso.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Study population described elsewhere (ref 29,30) but concerns host genetics, not drug-resistance data of interest.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary drug-resistance data referenced; paper does not study parasite markers.

assessment.summary: Longitudinal family-based study in Bobo-Dioulasso, Burkina Faso examining association of human hemoglobin C with reduced P. falciparum parasitemia and mild malaria attack. It reports host hemoglobin genotype frequencies, not any P. falciparum drug-resistance markers.
exclusion_reasons: Does not report any curated P. falciparum drug-resistance markers
No extractable parasite drug-resistance frequencies — only human hemoglobin allele data
